topic: Internal API platform architecture and data flow
reader: engineering manager evaluating whether the platform can absorb a new integration
use_context: A concise technical brief that explains the current architecture, constraints, and integration implications.
known_failure_modes: collapsing architecture into generic product language
prohibited_overclaims: does not claim verified performance beyond the documented scope
does not present roadmap assumptions as shipped behavior
does not overstate compatibility with unnamed systems

source_packets.title: Platform architecture note
source_packets.summary: Describes service boundaries, request flow, and the main data stores used by the platform.

findings.statement: The platform routes integration traffic through a gateway layer before dispatching to domain services.
findings.decision_note: Use this as the backbone of the overview and keep it precise.
failure_modes: flattening the gateway and service layers into one component
misunderstandings: assuming the gateway owns business logic

findings.statement: Batch jobs and event handlers are the main non-request path entry points into the system.
findings.decision_note: Mention these separately so the reader sees the asynchronous surface area.
failure_modes: omitting background processing from the overview
misunderstandings: equating request traffic with total system load

source_packets.title: Integration runbook excerpt
source_packets.summary: Summarizes integration prerequisites, retry behavior, and operational limits.

findings.statement: Clients must handle retries because transient downstream failures are expected.
findings.decision_note: This is a key caution for any adoption recommendation.
misunderstandings: interpreting retries as proof of durable delivery

findings.statement: The documented timeout budget is the main constraint on request chaining.
findings.decision_note: Use this to limit any implied end-to-end orchestration claims.
misunderstandings: assuming chained calls can ignore timeout budgets
